Co-authored-by: leejsinclair <707675+leejsinclair@users.noreply.github.com>

Reduce scheduled evaluation from every 3h to daily (#362)

* Reduce scheduled evaluation from every 3h to daily, skip if no changes

The evaluation schedule was consuming ~/5 days in Copilot premium
requests from scheduled runs alone (~/month pace). This change:

1. Reduces the cron schedule from every 3 hours (8 runs/day) to once
   daily at 08:00 UTC. This alone saves ~87% of scheduled evaluation
   cost.

2. Adds a check-for-changes guard that skips the scheduled run entirely
   if there have been no new commits to main since the last successful
   scheduled evaluation. This avoids wasting tokens re-evaluating
   identical code.

The PR trigger and all PR evaluation behavior is unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: harden skip-if-no-changes guard

- Add actions: read permission so the workflow run lookup succeeds
- Use status=completed with jq conclusion filter instead of status=success
- Make all gh api calls non-fatal (fallback to has_changes=true on error)
- Make compare API call best-effort for logging only

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Scope actions:read to discover job, increase per_page to 100

- Move actions:read from workflow-level to discover job permissions
  for least-privilege (only the schedule guard needs it)
- Increase per_page from 10 to 100 so the skip guard finds the last
  successful run even after long failure streaks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Key skip guard off most recent completed run, not just successful

Only skip when the most recent completed scheduled run was successful
AND at the same SHA. If it failed/cancelled at the current SHA, proceed
to retry. This prevents the guard from skipping indefinitely after a
transient failure.

Also simplifies to per_page=1 since we only inspect the latest run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix jq filter to yield empty when no prior runs exist

Use // empty to guard against null when workflow_runs array is empty,
preventing 'null null' from leaking into LAST_SHA/LAST_CONCLUSION.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Bypass skip guard on manual reruns (run_attempt > 1)

When a maintainer clicks 'Re-run jobs' on a scheduled run, the event
is still schedule with the same SHA. Without this check, the guard
would skip the rerun if the previous attempt succeeded. Now reruns
always proceed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: update arxiv papers [2026-03-14 02:22:29 UTC]

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix climber gameplay: PAD constants, enemy sprites, blue platforms (#163)

* Fix climber gameplay: PAD constants, enemy sprites, blue platforms

- Fix PAD_UP (0x08->0x10) and PAD_DOWN (0x04->0x20) to match NES controller spec
- Differentiate enemy sprites from player using ai!=0 check (enemies use
  playerRJump/playerLJump tiles E8-EB, player keeps D8-DB tiles)
- Set attribute tables to palette 1 (0x55) for blue platform colors matching
  the cc65 reference ROM
- Update test DLLs and verified.bin snapshot
- Add ANESE screenshot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix attribute table address and simplify enemy walking sprites

- Use 0x2BC0 (nametable C) instead of 0x27C0 (nametable B) for second
  attribute table fill, matching NTADR_A/NTADR_C usage in stage rendering
- Simplify enemy WALKING to always use jump metasprite (playerRJump/
  playerLJump) since enemies only move left/right
- Update test DLLs and verified.bin

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use built-in PAD enum instead of redefining constants

- Remove local PAD_A/PAD_LEFT/PAD_RIGHT/PAD_UP/PAD_DOWN constants
- Use PAD.A, PAD.LEFT, PAD.RIGHT, PAD.UP, PAD.DOWN from neslib Enums.cs
- Change joy from byte to PAD type (pad_poll already returns PAD)
- Cast to (byte)PAD.LEFT for enemy AI random byte comparison
- Update copilot-instructions.md to never redefine built-in constants

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Match climber.c draw_actor: same sprites for all actors, add regression tests

- Revert ai!=0 enemy sprite differentiation — original climber.c uses
  identical metasprites for all actors, differentiated only by palette
- Add PadPollUpDown_CorrectAndValues test: verifies PAD.UP emits AND
  #\ and PAD.DOWN emits AND #\ (not 0x08/0x04 START/SELECT)
- Add VramFillAttributeTable_CorrectAddresses test: verifies vram_adr
  uses \ and \ (not \) for attribute table fill

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix _padPollResultAvailable not cleared after non-pad_poll calls

The AND handler reloads from _padReloadAddress when _padPollResultAvailable
is true, which is correct for pad_poll results. However, the flag was never
cleared after other calls like rand8(), causing subsequent AND operations
(e.g., enemy direction checks: ej & 0x40) to incorrectly reload from the
player's joy address instead of the enemy's local variable.

Fixed by clearing _padPollResultAvailable in the call return value handler
for any non-pad_poll call. pad_poll sets its own flag before this code runs,
so the check is safe.

Added regression test PadPollAndRand8_IndependentAndOperations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: differentiate enemy/player sprites, rename test

Enemies now use jump sprites for STANDING/WALKING states while the
player keeps stand/run animations, matching the reference ROM behavior.

Renamed PadPollUpDown_CorrectAndValues → PadPollUpDown_UsesCorrectAndImmediateOperands
for clarity.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix dup cascade stale register bug and add regression test

When an intervening ldelem+stloc (e.g., byte nm = names[idx]) occurs
between a value load and its dup cascade (e.g., if (st == 1) ... if (st == 2)),
the stloc clears _runtimeValueInA. The dup handler now scans backward
for STA TEMP () and emits LDA  to reload the value.

Also fixes EmitBranchCompare to handle LDA ZeroPage with ImmediateOperand
(ZeroPage mode creates ImmediateOperand, not AbsoluteOperand).

This fixes the climber sample where CMP #01 compared isEnemy instead of
st (actor state), causing the player sprite to not render.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix EmitBranchCompare stripping runtime SBC/ADC instructions

When _runtimeValueInA is true, WriteLdc returns without emitting LDA #imm.
EmitBranchCompare's default path blindly called RemoveLastInstructions(1),
which removed the preceding computation (SBC, ADC, AND) instead of an LDA.

This caused ladder climbing proximity checks like:
  (byte)(actor_x[pi] - ladx) < 16
to lose the SBC instruction, making the check always fail.

Fix: only remove the last instruction if it's actually LDA Immediate.

Also adds SubThenCompare_RuntimeSubNotStripped regression test and
updates climber verified.bin snapshot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add nametable attribute computation for correct ladder/item colors

After floor rendering, briefly turn PPU off to write nametable attributes
via direct VRAM access. Floor surface rows (dy <= 1) use palette 1 (teal
blocks), all other rows use palette 0 (yellow ladders/items).

This replaces the TODO that noted vrambuf_put with runtime-filled arrays
caused stack leaks. The fix avoids vrambuf entirely by using ppu_off +
vram_adr/vram_put for direct writes during level setup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix WriteStloc removing LDA Absolute for local-to-local copy

When writing stloc for a scalar local (byte), WriteStloc's optimization
would RemoveLastInstructions(1) and re-emit LDA #constant. This was
correct when the previous instruction was LDA Immediate (from WriteLdc),
but wrong when it was LDA Absolute (from WriteLdloc loading another local).

The fix checks LastLDA before removing: if the last instruction was not
LDA Immediate, just emit STA without removing the previous LDA.

This fixes ladder climbing in the climber sample where 'lx = ladx'
(local-to-local copy) was always storing 0 instead of the runtime value.

Adds regression test LdlocStloc_LocalToLocalCopy that verifies the
body of a conditional local-to-local copy emits LDA Absolute (AD xx xx)
rather than LDA Immediate (A9 00).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix dup cascade stale flag and ushort hi-byte extraction

Two bugs fixed:

1. _dupCascadeActive stale flag: after a dup cascade (if-else chain),
   the flag stayed true, causing later non-cascade dups to incorrectly
   emit LDA \ (TEMP_HI). Fix: clear the flag when IsDupCascadeStart()
   returns false for a subsequent dup.

2. Ushort hi-byte extraction (dup+shr pattern): when extracting lo/hi
   bytes from a ushort (dup; conv_u1; stloc lo; ldc 8; shr; stloc hi),
   the shr handler didn't know X still held the high byte from the
   original ushort computation. Fix: track via _dupPreservedUshortHi
   flag, emit TXA instead of 8 LSR instructions.

Both bugs caused floor_yy_hi and ceil_yy_hi to be 0 instead of the
correct values, preventing ladder climbing from working.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: rename isEnemy to actorType, use nameof() for pad_poll

- Rename isEnemy to actorType in climber sample (reflects 3-state value: player/enemy/rescue)
- Replace string literal 'pad_poll' with nameof(NESLib.pad_poll) for maintainability

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Trigger CI

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix player sprite rendering: inline actor_name[ai] to avoid IL stack interleaving

The C# compiler interleaved the actor_name[ai] load between the
actor_state[ai] load and its dup cascade, causing the transpiler to
compare the wrong value (actorType instead of st) against STANDING.

Fix: use actor_name[ai] inline inside each if block instead of
pre-computing to a local variable, avoiding the problematic IL pattern.

Also rebuild test DLLs from updated Program.cs (11776 -> 12288 bytes)
and update verified.bin snapshot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: resolve pushax address when decsp4 is active

The Ldstr handler, WriteLdc (ushort), and WriteLdloc emitted
JSR pushax without adding pushax to UsedMethods. When decsp4
was active (e.g. oam_spr, pad_poll), pushax was excluded from
the ROM, leaving JSR pointing to address .

Add UsedMethods tracking for pushax and update the inclusion
condition in Program6502.AddFinalBuiltIns. This fixes pong,
sprites, and bigsprites samples.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add pong sample screenshot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Sync CalculateFinalBuiltInsSize with AddFinalBuiltIns pushax condition

CalculateFinalBuiltInsSize must mirror the needsPushaPushax logic
so totalSize (and donelib) are computed correctly when pushax is
needed via ldstr/ldloc with decsp4 active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix pong game logic: use transpiler-compatible patterns

Rewrote Program.cs to avoid IL patterns the transpiler doesn't handle:
- Single PAD variable with immediate store (fixes pad_poll(1) missing
  argument and pad2 using pad1's value)
- Direction flags with constant-speed if/else instead of variable-to-
  variable add (fixes ball_x/ball_y missing LDA before ADC)
- Correct serve direction after scoring (ball now moves away from the
  edge it exited, preventing immediate re-score)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update pong screenshot with fixed game

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Trigger CI

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix byte array state leak in transpiler default Call handler

After a byte array (e.g., PALETTE) is initialized via Ldtoken + InitializeArray,
the _lastByteArrayLabel state was never cleared because GetNumberOfArguments
returns 0 for BCL methods like InitializeArray. This caused
_needsByteArrayLoadInCall to remain true through subsequent stloc operations,
leading to spurious LDA/LDX byte array address instructions before unrelated
calls like oam_hide_rest.

The fix clears _lastByteArrayLabel and _needsByteArrayLoadInCall after the byte
array address is consumed by the first call that needs it (e.g., pal_all). Also
clears state in the oam_spr handler as a safety measure.

This fixes the pong sample where oam_hide_rest received a corrupted argument
(A=\ instead of oam_id), causing an infinite loop that set all sprite X
positions to 240 and prevented the game loop from advancing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix _lastByteArrayLabel leak and add missing UsedMethods pushax tracking

The _lastByteArrayLabel field leaked across Call instructions, causing
spurious LDA/LDX byte-array-address loads before unrelated calls like
oam_hide_rest. This made all sprites invisible (X=240, offscreen).

Fix: clear _lastByteArrayLabel in the oam_spr handler and in the
default Call handler's needsLoad block.

Additionally, several EmitJSR("pushax") call sites were missing the
corresponding UsedMethods?.Add("pushax") tracking. Without tracking,
Program6502.AddFinalBuiltIns excluded pusha/pushax subroutines from
the ROM when needsDecsp4 was active, causing JSR targets to resolve
to wrong addresses and ANESE to hit illegal opcode 0x80.

Added UsedMethods tracking to:
- Ldstr handler (ILDispatch.cs)
- WriteLdc ushort handler (LocalVariables.cs)
- WriteLdloc byte array label handler (LocalVariables.cs)
- WriteLdloc inline constant handler (LocalVariables.cs)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace pong.png with animated pong.gif and add recording script

Add pong_record.lua — a Mesen2 Lua script that simulates Player 1
controller input (tracking the ball) and captures PNG frames for GIF
assembly. The script reads sprite positions from OAM to drive the
paddle and captures every 4th frame for a ~15fps GIF.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix window Escape key, focus management, and input pipeline (#245)

* Fix: Escape key doesn't close windows opened from menus

When a window was opened via a menu item, focus was never transferred from
the menu bar to the new window. This happened because:

1. PopupStack.Clear() restored focus to the MenuNode (focusRestoreNode)
2. WindowPanelNode's focus management was blocked by ParentManagesFocus()
   (VStackNode.ManagesChildFocus = true) so it never set window focus
3. The input router's BuildPathToFocused found the focused MenuNode first,
   so WindowNode's intrinsic Escape binding was never reached

Fix:
- WindowPanelNode: When new windows are detected, bypass ParentManagesFocus
  and force focus transfer to the active window, clearing stale focus from
  menu nodes and other external sources
- WindowWidget: Clear existing focus before setting new window focus (as a
  fallback for cases where WindowPanel doesn't manage focus)
- FocusRing.Rebuild: Add safety net to resolve multiple focused nodes by
  keeping only the last one in tree order
- Un-skip OpeningWindowsViaMenu_SecondWindowGetsEscaped test
- Add FramelessWindow_OpenedViaMenu_ClosesWithEscape test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add escape key timeout for real terminal input

The input pump buffered bare ESC (0x1B) bytes indefinitely waiting
for ANSI sequence continuations. This prevented standalone Escape
key presses from ever being dispatched in a real terminal.

Add a 50ms timeout (standard threshold used by ncurses, crossterm,
etc.) that flushes buffered ESC as a bare Escape key event when no
continuation arrives. Uses Task.WhenAny to race the pending read
against the timer — the read is never cancelled, avoiding stdin
stream corruption on Linux.

Also adds a WindowFocusIntegrationTest with NotificationPanel
wrapper matching the exact WindowingDemo widget tree structure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add double-escape event pump survival test

Verifies that two consecutive bare ESC bytes (each flushed via
timeout) do not break the input pump, and that a subsequent
normal key is still dispatched correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: zero-alloc channel-based escape timeout with configurable duration

Replace Task.WhenAny/Task.Delay approach with channel-based architecture:
- Background reader posts raw data to Channel<PresentationInputEvent>
- Timer callback posts timeout sentinel to same channel
- Processing loop consumes from single channel (zero alloc hot path)

PresentationInputEvent is a readonly struct (no boxing), Channel.ReadAsync
returns pooled ValueTask, ITimer.Change reuses existing timer.

Add EscapeSequenceTimeout to Hex1bTerminalOptions (null=50ms default,
Zero=disabled). Expose via Hex1bTerminalBuilder.WithEscapeTimeout().

Tests: custom timeout, zero-timeout disabling, double-escape survival.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix window focus: use RequestFocusCallback for deferred focus

Replace direct SetNodeFocus calls during reconciliation with a deferred
RequestFocusCallback that runs after FocusRing.Rebuild with correct
focusable state. This fixes the issue where windows opened from menus
didn't receive focus because FocusRing had stale popup-era state.

Changes:
- Add RequestFocusCallback to ReconcileContext (threaded through all
  With* methods, same pattern as ScheduleTimerCallback)
- Wire callback in Hex1bApp.ReconcileAsync -> RequestFocus method
- Refactor WindowPanelNode to use callback for new window focus,
  targeting content focusables (not window frame or title bar)
- Simplify WindowWidget.ReconcileAsync (remove redundant focus code)
- Add end-to-end test proving TextBox in window receives focus
  immediately (type text, verify it appears, Escape closes)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restore focus to next window in z-order on close

When a window is closed, focus now returns to the next active window's
first content focusable instead of falling to the background (MenuBar).
This uses the same RequestFocusCallback mechanism as window open.

Changes:
- WindowPanelNode: detect hasClosedWindow alongside hasNewWindow,
  extend focus block to handle both cases via RequestFocusCallback
- 4 new exhaustive tests:
  - 2-window: close top → focus returns to underlying window TextBox
  - 3-window cascade: close C→B→A with typing verification at each step
  - Last window close: app doesn't hang, focus returns to background
  - Modal over non-modal: close modal → focus returns to base window

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Un-skip OpeningSecondWindow test, re-skip MenuBar arrow nav with root cause

- OpeningSecondWindow_FocusesSecondWindow: un-skipped, now passes
- MenuBar_ArrowNavigation_WorksWithWindowOpen: re-skipped with documented
  root cause (MenuBarNode.SetFocus creates stale IsFocused on MenuNode
  that confuses InputRouter when popup is open)
- Cleaned up debug diagnostics from test
- Filed #244 for Table_WhenFocused_ShouldShowTableLevelFocusIndicator

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move EscapeSequenceTimeout config from Hex1bTerminalBuilder to Hex1bAppOptions

The escape sequence timeout is a TUI-level concern, not a terminal builder
concern. Move the public API surface to Hex1bAppOptions where users configure
app behavior. The internal plumbing (Hex1bTerminalOptions property and
Hex1bTerminal implementation) remains unchanged.

- Add EscapeSequenceTimeout property to Hex1bAppOptions
- Wire it through Hex1bApp constructor when creating the internal terminal
- Remove WithEscapeTimeout from Hex1bTerminalBuilder

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove EscapeSequenceTimeout from Hex1bTerminalOptions

Move to a constructor parameter on Hex1bTerminal instead. The options bag
shouldn't own TUI-level config — Hex1bAppOptions is the public API surface
and it passes the value through to the terminal constructor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Keep EscapeSequenceTimeout on Hex1bTerminalOptions only

This is a byte-level ANSI parsing concern (disambiguating bare ESC from
escape sequences), not a TUI-level option. Keep it as a quiet property
on Hex1bTerminalOptions with no builder method. Remove from Hex1bAppOptions
and Hex1bTerminal constructor parameter.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix flaky CI tests: wait for visual state after window close

The ClosingTopWindow and ClosingModalWindow tests were flaky because
change. The flag is set in the Escape handler, but RequestFocusCallback
runs during the next render frame. On slow CI runners, typing characters
before that frame completes means keystrokes arrive before focus is
restored.

Fix: WaitUntil now checks that the closed window's title text has
disappeared from the snapshot, proving the render loop has completed
the close and focus restoration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Mitch Denny <mitch@mitchdeny.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(supervisor): send bridge heartbeat before terminal for fast workers

Workers that complete without emitting heartbeat events left the lease
in STARTING state. The terminal heartbeat then failed because
STARTING → COMPLETING is not a valid transition (only RUNNING/HEARTBEATING
→ COMPLETING is allowed).

Now the supervisor sends a non-terminal bridge heartbeat before the
terminal one. This advances the lease STARTING → RUNNING, then the
terminal heartbeat completes RUNNING → COMPLETING. When the lease is
already past STARTING (from regular stream heartbeats), the bridge
heartbeat is handled as a normal heartbeat progression.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow (#154)

* chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow

Manual workflow_dispatch that rolls back the VPS to any previously
released image version. Accepts a version string (e.g. 0.12.4 or v0.12.4),
strips the leading 'v' to match the Docker image tag convention, pulls
the pinned image on the VPS, retags it as :latest, and restarts via
docker compose — without touching .env or docker-compose.yml.

Shares the deploy-vps concurrency group to prevent concurrent
deploy/rollback races. Guarded to main branch only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add deploy-vps concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml

The rollback workflow used group: deploy-vps but no existing deploy
workflow shared that group, so concurrent deploy + rollback runs were
not actually blocked.

Adding the same concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml (the reusable
workflow called by both deploy.yml and release.yml) means all VPS
deployments now share the group with rollback.yml, enforcing real
mutual exclusion.

Addresses PR review comment: concurrency group not shared with existing deploy workflows

commit.message:
fix(supervisor): transition lease LEASED → STARTING before heartbeat loop

The worker supervisor now transitions the lease from LEASED to STARTING
after spawning the worker process, before the heartbeat streaming loop
begins. Previously the lease stayed in LEASED state and heartbeats were
rejected because the heartbeat service only accepts STARTING, RUNNING,
or HEARTBEATING states.

Adds LeaseTransitionerPort to WorkerSupervisorDependencies (optional,
for backward compatibility) and wires the TransitionService adapter
in the automation runtime.
